_comment: QLoRA fine-tuning job config — PLACEHOLDER. Extends lora_job_template.json for 4-bit quantized base.
_phase: Phase 3 (future). Use this when training hardware has less than 16 GB VRAM.

job.name: theorc-boss-gemma4-qlora-v1
job.description: QLoRA adapter for gemma4:12b boss planning role — 4-bit quantized base for low-VRAM training

base_model._qat_preferred: When unsloth/gemma-4-12b-it-qat is available on HF, prefer it here — QAT base + QLoRA training gives better final quality than standard Q4 base.

quantization_config._nf4_note: NF4 (Normal Float 4) is specifically optimized for normally-distributed neural network weights. Always prefer nf4 over fp4 for LLM fine-tuning.
quantization_config._double_quant_note: Double quantization quantizes the quantization constants themselves, saving ~0.4 bits/param with negligible quality loss. Recommended.

adapter._qlora_note: QLoRA is LoRA applied to a 4-bit quantized base. The adapter weights themselves are stored in bf16; only the frozen base is 4-bit.

training._unsloth_qlora_note: Unsloth's QLoRA implementation is 2x faster than standard PEFT QLoRA and uses ~40% less VRAM via their custom CUDA kernels. Required for 12 GB VRAM training.

hyperparams._lr_note: Same as LoRA — 4-bit quantization doesn't require different LR.
hyperparams._batch_note: Reduced from LoRA's 2 to fit 12 GB VRAM.
hyperparams._paged_optimizer_note: Paged AdamW offloads optimizer states to CPU RAM when they don't fit in VRAM. Required for 12 GB VRAM setups.
hyperparams._gradient_checkpointing_note: Trades compute for memory — recomputes activations on backward pass instead of storing them. ~30% slower but reduces VRAM by ~40%.

output._gguf_merge_note: QLoRA adapter must be merged into base weights before GGUF export. Unsloth handles this automatically via model.save_pretrained_merged().

hardware._12gb_note: RTX 3080 (12GB), RTX 4070Ti (12GB), RTX 3060 (12GB) are viable. Will be slow (~3-4 hours for 300 examples).
hardware._ram_note: 32 GB system RAM recommended. Paged optimizer may need to offload states — 16 GB RAM minimum.

vs_lora._summary: Use QLoRA when VRAM < 16 GB. Quality is marginally lower (~1-2 rubric points) but practical on consumer hardware.
vs_lora.quality_gap: negligible for planning tasks (not math or code generation)
vs_lora.speed_ratio: QLoRA is ~30% slower than LoRA due to dequantization overhead
vs_lora.prefer_qlora_when: VRAM < 16 GB or cost is a concern
